text: Your gut and brain are in constant conversation — through nerves and chemistry. This connection, called the gut-brain axis, influences your mood and health more than you think. The vagus nerve acts like a highway, sending signals between your gut and brain. Emerging research shows that microbial metabolites from your gut can affect your mood. But, be cautious—these microbiome-mood claims are still in early stages of research. What if your feelings are influenced by tiny bacteria? Mind-blowing, right? Curious to learn more? Like and follow for more mind-bending science!
